diary:
Started the day checking model runs on the porch with coffee—overcast skies always make me think about atmospheric pressure systems. Drove into the U-District farmers market and spent a couple hours wandering between vendor stalls, picking up gorgeous vegetables and local honey. The vendors were chatty; Mrs. Chen asked about my research again. Grabbed a bagel at Blazer and just... sat there watching sparrows flit between tables. Needed that stillness.

Walked over to the lab in the afternoon to check on the experiments. The data's looking promising, which lifted my mood considerably. Drove home listening to a policy podcast that got me thinking about implementation gaps. Cooked up those farmers market vegetables for dinner—the freshness of it felt meaningful somehow. Ended the evening with tea and a book about bird migration patterns. There's something grounding about a quiet Saturday spent between work and home.
thought_process: I deliberately chose the U-District market today because it's close to campus and I wanted to consolidate my errands—efficiency mixed with pleasure. The overcast weather actually felt restorative rather than gloomy; it matched my internal rhythm. I lingered at Blazer longer than planned because I realized I've been running on fumes lately, pushing hard on the climate models. The podcast on the drive home stirred some anxiety about policy timelines, but cooking with those fresh vegetables grounded me again. I'm learning that my best thinking happens when I'm not forcing it—in the gaps between tasks. The book choice reflected that; bird migration is data, but it's also poetry, and I needed both today.
world_event_prompt: Overcast Saturday, high of 64F. Farmers markets in Ballard, Fremont, and the U-District in full swing. Mariners home game at T-Mobile Park at 1pm brings SoDo crowds.
